description: Machine-readable CAAS Bastion requirement map. Each row binds a known review gap to its artifact(s), its enforcing gate, a status, an explicit residual risk, and the date the binding was last verified. Consumed by audit_gate.py --external-audit-replacement (P21) to verify that every closed gap maps to a live, callable gate (or is an explicitly documented residual) rather than only to prose.

sla.note: last_verified ages on purpose: a Bastion requirement binding that has not been re-verified within the SLA window is surfaced (WARN) and eventually blocks (FAIL). Refresh by re-confirming each row's artifact+gate and bumping last_verified.

status_legend.gated: Closed AND enforced by a dedicated executable gate (an audit_gate.py sub-check or a standalone ci/*.py gate script) that verifies behavior/content, not just presence.
status_legend.presence_only: Closed deliverable whose enforcement is presence of the published document; no behavioral gate applies because the deliverable is a stance/methodology/spec narrative. Presence is enforced here by P21.
status_legend.documented_residual: An explicitly accepted/deferred residual risk (e.g. out-of-scope), tracked in docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md. residual_risk MUST be non-empty.

requirements.claim: Reviewer-facing CAAS document set required to replace the verifiable parts of an external audit
requirements.residual_risk: Reviewer/reference document set; presence-gated by P21. These are narrative deliverables with no behavioral sub-gate. The bundle artifacts (EXTERNAL_AUDIT_BUNDLE.*) are additionally verified by ci/verify_external_audit_bundle.py.

requirements.claim: STRIDE threat model per exported ABI surface
RNG entropy source enumeration + attestation methodology
requirements.residual_risk: Attestation methodology document; presence-gated. The OS RNG path is validated by the integrator per the doc; no behavioral audit_gate sub-check is wired for the methodology narrative itself.

Power/EM/microarchitectural-injection and fault-injection side channels are explicitly out of scope (deferred to operating-environment auditors) and tracked as a permanent residual in docs/RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md (gated by audit_gate.py --residual-risk-register). The methodology to lift the deferral (dudect on silicon, DPA workstation, T-test thresholds) is documented in the artifact.

requirements.claim: Cross-implementation interop matrix (libsecp256k1 / OpenSSL / Botan / Bouncy Castle / Bitcoin Core)
requirements.residual_risk: Published interop stance; presence-gated. Per-cell PASS rows reference reproducible test vectors in the document; no dedicated audit_gate sub-check aggregates them.

requirements.claim: Spec-clause to test-ID traceability (BIP-32/39/340/341/352, RFC 6979, FROST/MuSig2)
Compliance stance (FIPS/NIST/CC explicit non-claim + BIP conformance claims)
requirements.residual_risk: Compliance stance document; presence-gated. Non-claims are descriptive; the 'DO claim' rows are backed by G-5 spec-traceability evidence. No behavioral sub-check.

Behavioral gate is audit_gate.py --ct-tool-agreement. Artifact-based consumption of real per-tool verdict JSONs (fail-closed on a single PASS + SKIP) is ci/ct_independence_check.py; freshness wiring is Bastion B11.

requirements.claim: Citable protocol spec (algorithm suite + per-surface contracts)
requirements.residual_risk: Publishable spec document; presence-gated. Per-clause conformance is gated by G-5 (--spec-traceability); this row enforces the citable master document exists.

requirements.claim: Exploit <-> threat-model <-> spec traceability join (no in-scope high-severity clause without a PoC or residual)
Security disclosure SLA + incident timeline + RFC 9116 security.txt
External integration evidence freshness (libbitcoin / Bitcoin Core / libsecp shim) machine-tracked and gated
requirements.residual_risk: Heavy full-chain libbitcoin / Bitcoin Core surfaces are owner_gated within the manifest (explicit, never counted as current evidence); they are re-verified by an owner-imported artifact, not on every push.
